rows.locks: Eligible source-project glTF imports declarative 2.5D presentation geometry, material references, and orthographic camera settings into a Rust-owned native scene without a foreign runtime bridge or trusted Studio writes.

Unsupported glTF extensions, custom shader notes, behavior-bearing logic, physics, shader, and VFX markers remain Yellow/Red fidelity gaps or Era R re-derivation tasks and must never be silently graded clean/Green.

The report validator rejects fidelity rows that claim ported, auto-port, auto-ported, or auto-translated units, so an ungated finished-game auto-port claim fails until later captured oracle evidence passes.
The report validator recomputes the canonical nativeScene state-hash primary and rejects stale or tampered hashes; perceptual rendering remains secondary corroboration only.
Coverage v83 records the M97 glTF regression suite, scripted demo, Rust data-plane evidence boundaries, open #1/#23 governance anchors, and the exact verification command shape from #2195.

boundary: Scenario Coverage v83 is a Rust data-plane regression suite for Era P M97 glTF geometry and orthographic-camera import. It locks source-project/open-text glTF 2.0 presentation import, one-way import to Ouroforge-native skeleton evidence, no foreign runtime bridge, no auto-port or ported claim without a passing oracle, honest Yellow/Red fidelity gaps for lossy imports, deterministic state-hash primary behavior including stale-hash rejection, perceptual render secondary-only corroboration, and Rust-owned artifact truth. Elixir/Phoenix Studio is not touched and has no trusted write or artifact semantics authority. #1 and #23 remain open.
